Kali Linux integration test: 7/7 endpoints PASS. 3 bugs found and fixed.
Kali Linux container, SSH root:kali, Xvfb on :1, import+scrot installed

Phase E Commit 1: runOutbound() uses min*2^(attempt-1) capped at max, with math/rand/v2 jitter. Defaults: MaxRetries=0 (infinite), BackoffMin=1s, BackoffMax=60s. CLI flags: --max-retries, --backoff-min, --backoff-max.

Commit 5/5 complete: reconnect hardening (a0a20fd) + Windows real PowerShell (d16dff3) + macOS real (438ebc7) + rate limiting (Commit 4) + health monitoring (Commit 5). Remaining: TLS mutual auth, token rotation.

Phase E Commit 4: RateLimiter in internal/server/proxy.go (stdlib sync+time only). Per-agent tokenBucket map, Allow(agentID)/Release(), defaults 10 req/s burst 20 max 5 concurrent. LLMProxy.Call signature changed to Call(agentID, prompt); returns provider 'rate_limited' when denied. Server gains rateLimit field + NewServerWithRateLimit(addr,token,registry,RateLimitConfig). CLI flags: --rate-limit (10), --rate-burst (20), --max-concurrent (5). 6 unit tests in proxy_test.go all pass. Zero new deps.

Phase E Commit 5: Extended AgentRecord with UptimeSeconds, LastError, ErrorCount, HealthScore (0.0-1.0 composite), ResourceUsage *ResourceInfo. NEW ResourceInfo struct (CPUPercent/MemoryMB/DiskFreeMB). Health score formula: heartbeat recency (0.0-0.4) + error count (0.0-0.3) + uptime stability (0.0-0.3). NEW Registry methods: RecordError, UpdateHealth, GetHealth, StartStaleDetector (idempotent), Stop (idempotent via stopCh+sync.Once). Stale detector goroutine scans every 30s, marks agents with heartbeat > 90s as 'stale'. Started in NewServer, stopped in Close (no leak). NEW GET /api/agent/{id}/health endpoint (404 for unknown). Enhanced /health returns {status,total_agents,active_agents,stale_agents,uptime_seconds}. Error recording wired into handleMessages (TypeError→RecordError, TypeHealthResult→UpdateHealth). Internal connectedAt/lastHeartbeat time.Time fields excluded from JSON, reconstructed from string fields on load. Zero new deps. Build+vet+tests pass.

Phase E Commit 2: 6 stubs replaced with PowerShell-based real implementations. Screenshot (System.Drawing→PNG), ScreenInfo (AllScreens), Click (Cursor+user32.dll), TypeText/KeyPress/Hotkey (SendKeys), Notify (msg.exe+ToastNotificationManager). ScreenStreamStart/Stop remain stubs. Zero new deps. Cross-compile passes.

Phase E Commit 3: NEW platform_darwin.go implements all 25 Platform interface methods via macOS-native CLI tools (zero new deps). Screenshot (screencapture -x -t png -), ScreenInfo (system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType), Click/TypeText/KeyPress/Hotkey (osascript AppleScript + key-code map), Clipboard (pbpaste/pbcopy), OpenURL (open), Notify (osascript display notification), ProcessList (ps -axo pid,comm,pcpu,rss), ProcessKill (syscall.Kill), Exec (bash -c with cmd.Process.Kill timeout). ScreenStreamStart/Stop remain stubs (Phase F). Added explicit //go:build linux tag to platform_linux.go. Cross-compile passes for darwin/linux/windows; go vet passes for all three.

cmd/server.purpose: Server binary — WebSocket server + LLM proxy

internal/server.purpose: Multi-session WebSocket server, agent registry, LLM proxy

internal/agent.purpose: Agent loop, connection management, command dispatch

tool.purpose: Hermes plugin registering 5 remote tools for operative profile

gotchas.detail: The initial scaffold used 'falke-remote'. All imports must use the full GitHub path. go.mod was fixed in commit 3ab97c9.

gotchas.rule: Go 1.22.5 is not a system package
gotchas.detail: Installed at /opt/data/go/bin/go from go.dev. Must export PATH=/opt/data/go/bin:$PATH before building. System Go (if installed) may be older.

gotchas.rule: gorilla/websocket is the only external dependency
gotchas.detail: Zero other dependencies. The binary should stay minimal. Add new deps only with explicit justification in PR description.

gotchas.rule: Agent connect URL must include /ws path
gotchas.detail: Fixed in commit dc02281. If the user provides a URL without /ws (e.g., wss://host:7700), the agent appends /ws automatically. The server's WebSocket upgrade handler is at /ws.
The agent verifies the server's TLS certificate fingerprint on first connect. For testing, use --insecure flag to skip verification. Production should use proper CA-signed certs.

gotchas.rule: Platform tools must be installed on target
gotchas.detail: Linux: xdotool (click/type/key), import or scrot (screenshot), xclip (clipboard). macOS: osascript (screenshot), pbcopy/pbpaste (clipboard). Windows: PowerShell, .NET screen capture. Missing tools cause command timeouts, not clear errors.

gotchas.rule: Screenshot requires DISPLAY env + Xvfb on headless hosts
gotchas.detail: import/scrot need DISPLAY set and an X server running. On headless hosts (Kali container, CI), start Xvfb first: `Xvfb :1 -screen 0 1024x768x24 &`. Server must pass os.Environ() to screenshot commands. Fixed in b25a852.

gotchas.rule: Server --addr flag must be wired in main.go
gotchas.detail: The server binary's main.go must use flag.String for --addr, --token, --registry with env var fallback. Without this, --addr is silently ignored and server always binds localhost:7700. Fixed in b25a852.

gotchas.rule: import defaults to PostScript when piping to stdout
gotchas.detail: ImageMagick's `import -window root -` produces PostScript by default. Use `import -window root png:-` to force PNG output. Fixed in b25a852.

gotchas.rule: Platform files are constrained by BOTH filename suffix AND build tag
gotchas.detail: platform_linux.go / platform_windows.go / platform_darwin.go are automatically GOOS-restricted by their _linux/_windows/_darwin filename suffixes. Phase E Commit 3 added explicit //go:build linux tag to platform_linux.go as belt-and-suspenders (the other two already had tags). Each GOOS picks up exactly platform.go + its platform_<os>.go file, which defines the New() factory. Without the correct file for a target GOOS, the agent package fails with 'undefined: platform.New'.

gotchas.rule: macOS ProcessList parses ps -axo from the end (comm contains spaces)
gotchas.detail: macOS `ps -axo pid,comm,pcpu,rss` — the comm column can contain spaces (e.g. '/path to/app'), so platform_darwin.go parses fields from the end: last field is rss (KB→MB), second-to-last is pcpu (float), field[0] is pid, and the middle is the joined name. Do not naively Sscanf the whole line.

gotchas.rule: LLMProxy.Call signature changed to Call(agentID, prompt) in Phase E Commit 4
gotchas.detail: Phase E Commit 4 added per-agent rate limiting. LLMProxy.Call now takes agentID as its first argument for per-agent token-bucket limiting. Any caller still using the old Call(prompt) signature will fail to compile. NewLLMProxy() still works unchanged (it creates a default RateLimiter). Use NewServerWithRateLimit(addr, token, registry, RateLimitConfig) to configure limits from CLI flags; NewServer(addr, token, registry) uses proxy defaults (10 req/s, burst 20, max 5 concurrent).

gotchas.rule: AgentRecord has unexported time.Time fields excluded from JSON serialization
gotchas.detail: Phase E Commit 5 extended AgentRecord with internal connectedAt time.Time and lastHeartbeat time.Time fields (json:"-"-style via unexported fields). These are NOT serialized to JSON; they are reconstructed from the exported string fields (ConnectedAt, LastHeartbeat, both RFC3339) in Registry.load(). Any code that constructs an AgentRecord literal without setting connectedAt/lastHeartbeat will have zero-time internal fields, so computeHealthScore and ListAgents' uptime computation will return 0 — always use Registry.Register/Heartbeat/UpdateHealth which set both the string and internal fields together. The /api/agent/{id}/health endpoint and ListAgents recompute UptimeSeconds + HealthScore on read from these internal fields.

gotchas.rule: Stale-detector goroutine is started in NewServer and must be stopped via Registry.Stop()
gotchas.detail: Phase E Commit 5 adds a background goroutine in Registry.runStaleDetector() that scans every 30s (staleCheckInterval) for agents with LastHeartbeat > 90s old (staleThreshold) and marks them 'stale'. It is started automatically by NewServer via reg.StartStaleDetector(). To avoid a goroutine leak, Server.Close() calls s.registry.Stop() which closes stopCh (idempotent via sync.Once). Any code that constructs a Registry directly (outside NewServer) and wants stale detection must call StartStaleDetector() explicitly; Stop() is safe to call even if StartStaleDetector was never called.

recent_changes.summary: Phase E Commit 5: Health Monitoring — extended AgentRecord with UptimeSeconds/LastError/ErrorCount/HealthScore/ResourceUsage + internal non-serialized time.Time fields. NEW ResourceInfo struct (CPUPercent/MemoryMB/DiskFreeMB). Health score formula (0.0-1.0): heartbeat recency (0.0-0.4) + error count (0.0-0.3) + uptime stability (0.0-0.3). NEW Registry methods: RecordError, UpdateHealth, GetHealth, StartStaleDetector (idempotent), Stop (idempotent via stopCh+sync.Once). Stale detector goroutine scans every 30s, marks heartbeat > 90s as 'stale'; started in NewServer, stopped in Close (no leak). NEW GET /api/agent/{id}/health endpoint (404 for unknown). Enhanced /health returns {status,total_agents,active_agents,stale_agents,uptime_seconds}. Error recording wired into handleMessages (TypeError→RecordError, TypeHealthResult→UpdateHealth with ResourceInfo). protocol.HealthResult extended with optional resource fields. Zero new deps. Build+vet+tests pass. Added gotchas HR-013, HR-014.

Phase E Commit 4: Rate Limiting — per-agent token-bucket rate limiter on the LLM proxy (stdlib sync+time only, zero new deps). RateLimiter struct with per-agent tokenBucket map, Allow(agentID)/Release(), configurable rate/burst/maxConcurrent (defaults 10 req/s, burst 20, max 5 concurrent). LLMProxy.Call signature changed to Call(agentID, prompt); returns 'rate_limited' provider marker when denied. SetRateLimiter() overrides defaults. Server gains rateLimit field + exported RateLimitConfig + NewServerWithRateLimit constructor. CLI flags: --rate-limit (10), --rate-burst (20), --max-concurrent (5). 6 unit tests in internal/server/proxy_test.go all pass. Build + vet + cross-compile (darwin/linux/windows × amd64/arm64) all exit 0.

Phase E Commit 3: macOS Real Implementation — NEW platform_darwin.go implements all 25 Platform interface methods via macOS-native CLI tools (zero new deps). Screenshot (screencapture -x -t png -), ScreenInfo (system_profiler), Click/TypeText/KeyPress/Hotkey (osascript AppleScript + key-code map), Clipboard (pbpaste/pbcopy), OpenURL (open), Notify (osascript display notification), ProcessList (ps -axo, parse-from-end for spaces in comm), ProcessKill (syscall.Kill), Exec (bash -c, cmd.Process.Kill timeout). ScreenStreamStart/Stop kept as stubs (Phase F). Added explicit //go:build linux tag to platform_linux.go. Cross-compile + vet pass for darwin/linux/windows. Added gotchas HR-010, HR-011.

Phase E Commit 2: Windows Real Implementation — replaced 6 stubs in platform_windows.go with PowerShell-based real implementations. Screenshot (System.Drawing→PNG), ScreenInfo (AllScreens parsing), Click (Cursor+user32.dll), TypeText/KeyPress/Hotkey (SendKeys), Notify (msg.exe+ToastNotificationManager). ScreenStreamStart/Stop kept as stubs. Added strconv/strings imports. Zero new deps. Cross-compile and vet pass cleanly.

Phase E Commit 1: Reconnect Hardening — replaced fixed 5s backoff with exponential backoff + jitter. Added Config fields (MaxRetries, BackoffMin, BackoffMax), computeBackoff() method, CLI flags (--max-retries, --backoff-min, --backoff-max). Build and vet pass cleanly.

Phase D complete: Kali Linux integration test — 7/7 endpoints PASS. 3 bugs found and fixed (screenshot env, process-list route, --addr flag). 4 gotchas added (HR-007–HR-009). Windows binaries cross-compiled. CODER.md rule #6 doc-update obligation encoded.
8 bugs fixed across 6 files. Go agent connects + all 4 endpoints verified. Plugin registers 5 remote_* tools. Ready for Phase D (integration test on remote host).
Initial scaffold — protocol, server, agent, platform, CLI, plugin. Builds and vets cleanly.
